explanation: Windows command line parsing (CommandLineToArgvW) allows arguments to be wrapped in quotes. Passing "/S" and "/C" executes identically but evades the exact literal substring match of '/S' and '/C' expected by the rule.

Many Windows built-in utilities accept hyphens (-) interchangeably with forward slashes (/) for command-line switches. Providing -S and -C bypasses the rule since it explicitly hardcodes the forward slash.
Depending on the SIEM backend translation, if the case-insensitive modifier is not applied by default during Sigma conversion, using lowercase /s and /c will bypass the exact uppercase string match.
